title: Doe frozen Doppler reference fixture manifest
description: Schema for `frozen-reference.manifest.json` files indexing a frozen Doppler reference fixture under `bench/fixtures/r3-1-31b-doppler-frozen/`. The fixture pins one Doppler reference inference run as the comparison target so downstream parity checks score against a stable artifact (different WebGPU adapters produce different greedy sequences for the same prompt; the fixture freezes one branch). The manifest cites: a transcript JSON, per-layer per-probe-point activation tensors as `.npy` files, and the fixture's aggregate digest (`fixtureDigest`) that downstream receipts cite as `referenceFixtureHash` (consumed by `bench/tools/_receipt_hash_guard.py`).

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this schema. Currently 1.

artifactKind.description: Receipt class identifier; used by aggregation tooling.

modelId.description: Model identifier the fixture was captured from (e.g. `gemma-4-31b-it-text-q4k-ehf16-af32`). Bound by the receipt-side hash chain; readers should refuse to compare against a fixture whose modelId differs from the receipt's modelId.

modelRevision.description: Optional upstream model revision pin (e.g. HuggingFace commit sha). Helps reviewers materialize the same identity locally.

dtypeProfile.description: Canonical lane dtype profile sourced from the source Doppler manifest's `quantizationInfo`. Required for new lanes (af16, abf16, ...) so validators can enforce a `--lane-key` match against `variantTag` and prevent cross-lane fixture confusion. Optional for pre-existing af32 fixtures captured before this contract landed; validators accept those permissively. The contract is documented in `bench/tools/_lane_dtype_profile.py`.

lmHead.description: LM head dtype (defaults to weights when absent).

variantTag.description: Canonical lane key denormalized from the per-role fields (e.g. 'q4k-ehf16-af32', 'q4k-ehf16-af16'). Used as the `--lane-key` match target.

promptText.description: Prompt text the fixture was captured against. Recorded so reviewers can reproduce; not used as a hash input.

tokenizedPromptHash.description: sha256 of the tokenized prompt bytes the reference run consumed.

decodeSteps.description: How many decode steps after prefill the transcript captures.

frozenAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which this fixture was frozen.

frozenReason.description: Free-form note explaining why this branch was frozen (e.g. 'WebGPU adapter X greedy branch picked as the comparison target — see bench/out/r3-1-webgpu-determinism-survey/').

fixtureDigest.description: Aggregate sha256 binding every cited artifact in this manifest into a single digest. Computed as sha256 of the canonical-JSON serialization of `{transcript, activations, firstTokenLogits}` with each artifact's path and per-artifact sha256 included. Downstream receipts cite this value as `referenceFixtureHash`; `bench/tools/_receipt_hash_guard.py` enforces the chain.

firstTokenLogits.description: Optional per-vocab logits .npy for the first decoded token. Used by attention-canary, full-prefill, and multi-token continuation steps.

activations.description: Per-layer activation snapshots at the four TSIR boundary points (`post_rmsnorm`, `post_qkv`, `post_attn`, `post_ffn`). Keyed by layer index (0-based string), then by probe-point name. The fixture is required to cover at least one (layer, probe-point) pair; the single-block parity contract calls for L=1 across all four probe points.

reviewerNotes.description: Optional reviewer-facing notes (how the fixture was produced, how to reproduce, why a particular branch was frozen).

path.description: Relative path from the manifest's directory to the artifact.

elemShape.description: Shape tuple recorded as a flat array of non-negative ints (e.g. [seq_len, hidden_dim]).
